blocks.body: Future Perfect: Forms and Use — will have + V3 for actions that will be complete by a certain moment or event in the future.

{"level":"B1–B2","intro":"In Future Perfect, we use will have + V3 to show an action that will be complete by a certain moment or event in the future.","rules":[{"label":"will have + V3","color":"emerald","text":"This is the basic pattern for all subjects.","example":"I will have finished. / She will have arrived."},{"label":"Future deadline","color":"blue","text":"The tense shows that the result will already be ready by a future limit.","example":"By Friday, we will have sent the report."},{"label":"Before another future event","color":"amber","text":"Future Perfect helps show that one action will finish earlier than another future event.","example":"By the time you call, I will have left."}]}

{"title":"1. Basic formula","intro":"Start with the pattern that works with every subject.","items":[{"label":"All subjects","title":"will have + V3","subtitle":"I will have finished. / She will have arrived. / They will have left."},{"label":"Short forms","title":"I'll have / she'll have / they'll have","subtitle":"We often use contractions in natural speech."},{"label":"Meaning","title":"completed before then","subtitle":"The result will already be ready by that time."},{"label":"V3 after have","title":"finished / done / gone / written","subtitle":"After will have, use only V3."}]}

{"title":"2. When we use Future Perfect","sections":[{"label":"By a deadline","color":"emerald","description":"Talk about something that will be complete by a certain time, date, or deadline.","examples":[{"en":"By Friday, I will have finished the presentation.","ua":"The presentation will already be ready by Friday."},{"en":"By tomorrow evening, they will have cleaned the flat.","ua":"The result will already be there by tomorrow evening."}]},{"label":"Before another future event","color":"blue","description":"Show that one future action will finish earlier than another one.","examples":[{"en":"By the time you arrive, I will have left.","ua":"My leaving will be complete before your arrival."},{"en":"She will have prepared everything before the guests arrive.","ua":"Preparation comes first, then the other event."}]},{"label":"A ready result","color":"amber","description":"The tense works well when the key idea is not the process, but the completed result.","examples":[{"en":"By next month, we will have saved enough money.","ua":"The needed amount will already be ready by then."},{"en":"He will have learned the route by then.","ua":"The knowledge will already be in place by that point."}]},{"label":"Looking back from a future point","color":"violet","description":"Sometimes we imagine a future point and look back at what will already be complete by then.","examples":[{"en":"Next June, they will have lived here for ten years.","ua":"By that future point, the period will already add up."},{"en":"In a month, I will have worked here for a year.","ua":"We imagine a finished total by a future point."}]}]}

{"title":"3. Quick patterns","intro":"See how the form works in typical sentences.","rows":[{"en":"I will have finished by 6 p.m.","ua":"will have finished","note":"result by a deadline"},{"en":"She will have left before the meeting.","ua":"will have left","note":"completion before another event"},{"en":"They will have built the bridge by next year.","ua":"will have built","note":"prediction of completion"},{"en":"By then, we will have learned a lot.","ua":"will have learned","note":"a ready result by a future point"}],"warning":"Future Perfect shows completion before a point in time. If the action will still be in progress then, Future Continuous may fit better."}

{"title":"4. Common mistakes","items":[{"label":"Mistake 1","color":"rose","title":"Using has instead of have after will.","wrong":"❌ She will has finished by noon.","right":"✅ She will have finished by noon."},{"label":"Mistake 2","color":"amber","title":"Using V1 instead of V3.","wrong":"❌ We will have finish the task by Friday.","right":"✅ We will have finished the task by Friday."},{"label":"Mistake 3","color":"sky","title":"Putting will in a time clause after by the time.","wrong":"❌ By the time you will arrive, I will have left.","right":"✅ By the time you arrive, I will have left."}]}

{"title":"5. Short summary","items":["Future Perfect = will have + V3.","The form is the same for all subjects.","After will have, use V3.","This tense shows that an action will be complete by a certain future moment or event."]}
